role_summary: Software engineering internship working on products used by billions. Rotations through teams like Google Cloud, Search, Maps, Android, or YouTube.
key_requirements: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or equivalent
Proficiency in C++, Java, Python, or JavaScript
Experience with data structures and algorithms
Minimum 2 years of university remaining after internship

suggested_projects.project_ref: Personal GitHub repos
suggested_projects.rationale: Shows practical coding ability beyond coursework

personal_note: Google SWE internship is the gold standard — apply early. Their process typically includes OA + 2 technical rounds.
notes: Opens applications ~July each year for Summer following. Strong OA (coding round) is the first gate.

role_summary: Build the infrastructure and products that connect billions of people. Work across Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, or Meta AI teams.
key_requirements: Bachelor's degree in CS or related field
Experience with at least one programming language
1+ year of university remaining after internship

suggested_projects.project_ref: Full-stack project with React backend/frontend
suggested_projects.rationale: Meta uses React heavily — shows framework familiarity

personal_note: Meta interns get a lot of autonomy. Their internship-to-FTE conversion rate is ~60%+.
notes: CoE (Coding Exercise) online assessment required before interview scheduling.

role_summary: Work on products like iOS, macOS, iCloud, or Apple Silicon. Build the software that powers billions of devices.
key_requirements: Solid understanding of algorithms and systems

suggested_projects.project_ref: iOS/macOS app or systems-level project
suggested_projects.rationale: Apple values low-level and systems work highly

personal_note: Apple's interview process is known for deep system design questions. They value craftsmanship.
notes: Applications open ~July-August. Strong preference for on-site in Cupertino or Austin.

role_summary: Build large-scale distributed systems. Work on AWS, e-commerce, Alexa, or internal tools used by millions.
key_requirements: Proficiency in Java, C++, Python, or similar
nice_to_haves: Previous internship on a production team

suggested_projects.project_ref: Distributed system or cloud-based project
suggested_projects.rationale: Amazon's scale is unmatched — show you can think big

personal_note: OA is HackerRank coding. The loop includes 5-6 interviews testing DSA + Leadership Principles.
notes: Hiring manager round after OA. Leadership Principles are critically weighted in evaluation.

role_summary: Work on Azure, Office, Windows, GitHub Copilot, or Xbox. Microsoft is known for excellent mentorship and work-life balance.
key_requirements: Proficiency in C#, Java, Python, or JavaScript

suggested_projects.project_ref: Cloud-native or productivity tool project
suggested_projects.rationale: Aligns with Microsoft's core business areas

personal_note: Microsoft has one of the best SWE internship cultures. They value growth mindset (vs. fixed mindset in interviews).
notes: OA is CoderPad-style coding round. Interview focuses on collaboration and systems thinking.

location: Santa Clara, CA / Austin, TX / Remote options vary
role_summary: Work on GPU architecture, CUDA, AI frameworks (PyTorch), or data center infrastructure. NVIDIA is at the forefront of AI hardware.
key_requirements: Understanding of parallel computing / GPU concepts
team_name: GPU Computing, AI Research, Data Center

suggested_projects.project_ref: ML project with PyTorch or CUDA kernel work
suggested_projects.rationale: Directly maps to NVIDIA's core domain

personal_note: Huge growth company — great for ML systems people. Interview includes deep technical questions on systems/parallel computing.
notes: NVIDIA is hot right now — apply early. They hire both SWE and ML internship roles.

role_title: Research Scientist Intern / AI Engineer Intern
role_summary: Work on building AGI — research or engineering roles in LLMs, reinforcement learning, robotics, or safety.
key_requirements: Master's or PhD preferred for research roles; strong BS for engineering

suggested_projects.project_ref: LLM fine-tuning or training project
suggested_projects.rationale: Shows you understand the core tech stack

personal_note: Extremely competitive — top 0.1%. Need strong research or systems background.
notes: They value deep technical depth over breadth. A single impressive project beats many mediocre ones.

role_summary: Build payment infrastructure powering the internet. Work on APIs, developer tools, fraud detection, or financial systems.
key_requirements: Understanding of web protocols and distributed systems

suggested_projects.project_ref: API-heavy project with clean service architecture
suggested_projects.rationale: Stripe's whole business is APIs — show you get developer experience

personal_note: Known for engineering excellence and strong engineering culture. Their OA is on their own platform.
notes: Their interview loop includes system design and deep coding rounds. Strong emphasis on developer experience thinking.

role_summary: Build real-time matching algorithms, mapping infrastructure, payments systems, or logistics optimization for ride-sharing and delivery.
key_requirements: Strong DSA skills (graph algorithms highly relevant)
Experience with backend languages (Go, Java, Python)

suggested_projects.project_ref: Algorithm-heavy project (graph/pathfinding)
suggested_projects.rationale: Uber's core tech is graph-based matching and routing

personal_note: Great for people who like algorithmic problems. Their engineering blog is excellent reading.
notes: OA uses DSA-focused questions. Strong emphasis on system design for large-scale real-time systems.

location: New York, NY / Boston / Remote options
role_summary: Build observability and monitoring platforms used by thousands of companies for cloud infrastructure management.
key_requirements: Proficiency in Go, Java, Python, or C++
team_name: Core Platform, Observability, Customer Success Tools

suggested_projects.project_ref: Monitoring/telemetry or cloud infrastructure project
suggested_projects.rationale: Datadog's core product is monitoring — show you understand the domain

personal_note: Fast-growing observability company with great culture. Their internship program is well-established.
notes: Good alternative to big tech if you prefer focused infrastructure work over consumer products.

role_summary: Remote research fellowship focusing on STEM education and AI in education. Work with researchers at CZI to study how technology impacts learning outcomes.
key_requirements: Interest in education technology / AI for good
Research background or demonstrated analytical skills
US citizenship or permanent residency (for certain tracks)

suggested_projects.project_ref: Any data analysis or ML project with societal impact angle
suggested_projects.rationale: CZI values the intersection of tech and social good

personal_note: Excellent prestige fellowship. Remote, 1-year term. Great for students interested in AI+education.
notes: Application includes essays + letters of recommendation. Very competitive on the research side.

compensation_note: $40,000 stipend + $15,000 hardware grant + Google AI research resources
role_summary: Annual fellowship for PhD students in CS and related fields working on AI/ML. Includes mentorship from Google researchers and access to cloud resources.
key_requirements: Enrolled in PhD program (CS, ML, or related)
personal_note: PhD-only — keep for later if you pursue grad school. Extremely prestigious in the field.
notes: Requires PhD enrollment. Mark as 'keep for later' for now but save this — it's gold for research careers.

location: Redmond, WA / NYC / Seattle / Remote options
role_summary: Research internship at MSR working on AI, systems, HCI, or theory. Publishable research expected.

suggested_projects.project_ref: Research-oriented project with paper/writeup component
suggested_projects.rationale: MSR expects publishable work — show research maturity

personal_note: MSR internships often lead to PhD offers or full-time roles. One of the best research programs in tech.
notes: Fall applications have later deadlines than summer. Great opportunity for remote/hybrid work options.

location: San Francisco / Remote options by team
role_summary: Build enterprise SaaS products — CRM, Slack integration, Einstein AI, or MuleSoft integration platforms.
team_name: Sales, Service, Slack, Einstein AI, MuleSoft

suggested_projects.project_ref: SaaS product or API platform project
suggested_projects.rationale: Salesforce's whole business is enterprise SaaS — show you get B2B

personal_note: Good balance of comp + culture. Their internship program is large and structured.
notes: OA includes DSA + system design questions. Strong enterprise focus — different from consumer tech.

role_summary: Build e-commerce infrastructure powering millions of stores. Work on Ruby/GraphQL APIs, payments, or the Shopify admin platform.

suggested_projects.project_ref: E-commerce or marketplace project with GraphQL/API
suggested_projects.rationale: Directly maps to Shopify's core tech stack and domain

personal_note: Fully remote-friendly company with strong engineering culture. Great work-life balance.
notes: OA is HackerRank coding round. Their tech interview covers Ruby/Rails specifically if you have that background.

role_summary: Work on music streaming infrastructure, recommendation algorithms, audio processing, or the creator platform.

suggested_projects.project_ref: Recommendation engine or media streaming project
suggested_projects.rationale: Directly maps to Spotify's core products

personal_note: Unique culture with focus on autonomy and data-driven decisions. Great if you're into audio/music.

role_summary: Remote open-source fellowship where you contribute to real organizations (Linux Foundation, Mozilla, etc.) under mentorship. 12-week terms.
team_name: Mozilla, Linux Foundation, Chef, NixOS, and more partners
personal_note: Excellent for building OSS portfolio and getting into open source. Remote + flexible timing = very accessible.
notes: Applications open ~monthly. Multiple cohorts per year. Great stepping stone to paid OSS roles.

location: Menlo Park / NYC / Remote options for research
role_summary: Research internship at FAIR working on foundational AI — language models, computer vision, multimodal learning, or robotics.
key_requirements: MS or PhD student (preferred) or exceptional undergrad
personal_note: Top-tier research program. Extremely competitive but the best name in AI research.
notes: FAIR internships often lead to PhD offers at top schools or industry research roles. Apply with strong research background.

role_summary: Scholarship program for underrepresented students in computing. Includes mentorship, internship pipeline, and career support.
key_requirements: Enrolled in CS or related degree
Underrepresented group (Black/African American, Hispanic/Latino, Native American)
team_name: Amazon Future Engineer Program (scholarship, not team-based)
personal_note: Check eligibility before applying — restricted to underrepresented groups in computing. Great if eligible.
notes: Scholarship + internship pipeline. Deadline ~October. Keep track but verify eligibility criteria.

role_summary: Build data platform tools for government and enterprise. Work on Palantir Gotham, Foundry, or Apollo platforms — complex data integration at scale.

suggested_projects.project_ref: Complex data processing or security-focused project

personal_note: Known for intense coding interviews and mission-driven work. High comp, high intensity.

role_summary: Work on foundational AI models at DeepMind — reinforcement learning, language models, multimodal systems. Engineering roles focus on training infrastructure and scaling ML workloads.

Proficiency in Python, TensorFlow or PyTorch

suggested_projects.project_ref: ML project with PyTorch + training at scale

location: San Francisco / Remote options for certain roles

Research mindset with attention to safety

role_summary: Build MLflow, Delta Lake, or Unity Catalog — the infrastructure layer for enterprise AI/ML workflows. Work on large-scale data processing and model management.

nice_to_haves: Delta Lake or lakehouse architecture knowledge
team_name: ML Platform / Core Platform / Unity Catalog

suggested_projects.project_ref: Large-scale data pipeline with ML integration

role_summary: Use AI for drug discovery — generative models for molecular design, protein folding prediction, target identification. They've brought an AI-discovered drug into clinical trials.

nice_to_haves: Drug discovery or medicinal chemistry background

role_summary: Work on Verily's life sciences AI projects — disease prediction from wearable data, medical imaging analysis, or population health modeling. Google's deep-tech health company.

role_summary: Build ML systems on real-world clinical data — electronic health records, cancer registries, and treatment patterns. Help drug discovery and oncology research at scale.

role_summary: Build computational biology platform for drug discovery. Recursion automates wet-lab experiments with AI to map biological space and find new therapeutics.

role_title: AI/ML Intern — Drug Discovery & Precision Medicine (Summer 2027)
